volume.title: Foreign Relations of the United States, 1993-2000, Volume XV, Wars in the Balkans, 1993-1995

lanes.title: Opening Review and Vance-Owen
lanes.focus: Capture how the new administration inherited Bosnia policy, tested diplomatic plans, and weighed lift-and-strike, no-fly-zone enforcement, humanitarian relief, and recognition issues.
mustFind: PC/DC summaries on early policy review

lanes.title: Lift-and-Strike, UNPROFOR, and Safe Areas
lanes.focus: Track the recurring decision loop around the arms embargo, NATO air power, UN protection, safe areas, and congressional pressure.
mustFind: Deputies and Principals Committee decision records
Congressional and public diplomacy pressure points

lanes.title: Federation and Contact Group Diplomacy
lanes.focus: Document the Washington Agreement, Contact Group diplomacy, pressure on Belgrade and Pale, and the Bihac crisis that forced renewed policy review.
mustFind: Contact Group ministerial and allied telcon records

lanes.title: 1995 Crisis Escalation
lanes.focus: Build the document spine for the January-to-July escalation: Bosnia/Croatia PC/DC meetings, hostage crisis, UNPROFOR withdrawal planning, Srebrenica, and allied coordination.
mustFind: PC/DC summaries and BTF support memoranda
Clinton memcons and telcons with allied leaders

lanes.title: Coercive Diplomacy and Dayton
lanes.focus: Follow the shift from crisis response to coercive diplomacy, including NATO air operations, Contact Group terms, shuttle diplomacy, and the Dayton proximity talks.
mustFind: Presidential calls with Izetbegovic, Tudjman, Milosevic, and allied leaders

lanes.title: Implementation Handoff
lanes.focus: Mark the transition from war termination to implementation: IFOR/NATO commitments, Paris signing, alliance presentation, and unresolved accountability issues.

sources.title: Official FRUS Volume XV Page
sources.description: The official landing page identifies the volume title and states that the volume is currently being researched.
sources.compilerUse: Use as the canonical title, date boundary, and public status reference for all downstream records.

sources.title: Status of the FRUS Series
sources.description: The production table lists the volume under Being Researched and places related 1993-2000 European volumes in the same production plan.
sources.compilerUse: Check whether the volume moves from research to clearance and flag adjacent volumes with overlapping evidence.

sources.title: Bosnia Collection
sources.description: NSC European Affairs files from Jane Holl and Donald Kerrick, covering Bosnia-related memoranda, talking points, letters, reports, press guidance, news articles, and State Department cables.
sources.compilerUse: Primary folder map for humanitarian aid, UNPROFOR, lift-and-strike, Vance-Owen, Carter cease-fire efforts, and 1993-1995 policy traffic.

sources.title: Bosnian Declassified Records
sources.description: More than 300 declassified records related to the Director of Central Intelligence Interagency Balkan Task Force and intelligence support for Bosnia policy.
sources.compilerUse: Pair intelligence products with PC/DC meetings, military option papers, and 1995 decision points.

sources.title: Alexander Vershbow - Bosnia
sources.description: NSC European Affairs files from 1994 to early 1996, including PC/DC summaries, UN Security Council decisions, intelligence-related records, and Carter cease-fire materials.
sources.compilerUse: Dense folder sequence for late-1994 Bihac, 1995 Bosnia/Croatia meetings, and the policy road into Dayton.

sources.title: Genocide in Yugoslavia
sources.description: Records on ethnic cleansing, Srebrenica, war-crimes accountability, reports, correspondence, talking points, cables, press guidance, and related memoranda.
sources.compilerUse: Build the Srebrenica and atrocity-response documentary trail and separate operational evidence from public messaging.

sources.title: PC/DC Meetings on Bosnia
sources.description: Mandatory Declassification Review release covering Principals Committee and Deputies Committee meetings on Bosnia from 1993 through 1995.
sources.compilerUse: Use summaries of conclusions as the decision backbone for every chapter lane.

sources.title: Clinton-Izetbegovic Memcons and Telcons
sources.description: Mandatory Declassification Review release with President Clinton conversations with Bosnian President Alija Izetbegovic from 1993 through 1995, plus a Milosevic pull-aside memcon.
sources.compilerUse: Anchor presidential diplomacy with Bosnian leadership and cross-check chronology against schedule records.

sources.title: Summer 1995 Dayton-Track Documents
sources.description: Mandatory Declassification Review release of summer 1995 Bosnia documents referenced in Getting to Dayton, including Lake, NSC, and State Department materials.
sources.compilerUse: Bridge the July crisis, current strategy review, and the diplomatic track that produced Dayton.

sources.title: Allied Leader Memcons and Telcons
sources.description: Mandatory Declassification Review release of Clinton conversations with Jacques Chirac, Helmut Kohl, and John Major on Bosnia in June-July 1995, plus a Christopher-de Charette telcon.
sources.compilerUse: Document alliance management after the hostage crisis, before and after Srebrenica, and during air-power negotiations.

sources.title: Declassified Documents Concerning Croatia
sources.description: Mandatory Declassification Review release with material on Croatia, including a strategy paper, a demarche to Milosevic, and a January 17, 1995 cable on a meeting with Milosevic.
sources.compilerUse: Add the Croatia/Krajina and Milosevic pressure track to the Bosnia-centered source base.

sources.title: Balkan Peace Agreement AV Finding Aid
sources.description: Photographs from the Balkan Peace Agreement meetings in Paris on December 14, 1995, including Clinton, Christopher, Holbrooke, Izetbegovic, Tudjman, Milosevic, and allied leaders.
sources.compilerUse: Verify Paris signing participants and support final-volume contextual apparatus.

sources.title: President Clinton's Daily Schedule
sources.description: Digitized presidential schedules for the Clinton administration.
sources.compilerUse: Verify meeting and call times before assigning date lines to memcons and telcons.

sources.title: Memcons - Memoranda of Conversation
sources.description: Collection-level index for declassified memoranda of face-to-face presidential and vice-presidential conversations.
sources.compilerUse: Use as the item-level entry point for meetings with Chirac, Major, NATO leaders, Yeltsin, and other foreign officials that intersect the Balkans story.

sources.title: Telcons - Memoranda of Telephone Conversation
sources.description: Collection-level index for declassified memoranda of telephone conversations with foreign leaders.
sources.compilerUse: Use as the item-level entry point for calls with Mitterrand, Chirac, Major, Kohl, Yeltsin, and other leaders during Bosnia policy review.

sources.title: Telcons and Memcons Folder
sources.description: A Bosnia collection folder titled Telcons and Memcons from NSC European Affairs files.
sources.compilerUse: Use as the folder-level check against item-level memcon/telcon records, especially for November 1994 and 1995 conversation packets.

sources.title: NARA Scout Memcon/Telcon Search
sources.description: Computer Use search run through NARA Scout against all Clinton administration collections, 1993-1995, narrowed to declassified digitized records and memcon/telcon plus Bosnia/Dayton/Balkans terms.
sources.compilerUse: Use as the NARA Catalog cross-check against the Clinton Library item pages; the useful declassified hit was the Clinton-Yeltsin Hyde Park memcon on Bosnia implementation.

sources.title: Strobe Talbott FOIA Case F-2017-13804
sources.institution: State Department FOIA Library / Strobe Talbott FOIA Research Desk
sources.description: Public manifest of 1,474 Talbott FOIA records with enriched descriptions and direct State Department PDF links. The manifest search found 67 Balkans hits: four in-volume contextual leads, no new 1993-1995 memcon/telcon candidates, and a larger post-1995 Kosovo/Bosnia follow-on cluster.
sources.compilerUse: Use for the Russia-facing diplomatic side channel around Kozyrev, Holbrooke, Dayton implementation, and the Volume XVI handoff; pull C09000009, C09000010, C09000068, and C09000051 first.

sources.title: Declassified Documents Collection
sources.description: Foreign-affairs MDR collection with Bosnia, no-fly-zone, endgame strategy, NATO, and related releases.
sources.compilerUse: Sweep for small MDR releases that do not sit cleanly inside the larger Bosnia collection.

chronology.title: Clinton administration inherits Bosnia policy
chronology.detail: Early transition and intelligence materials frame Bosnia, Croatia, Serbia, and the former Yugoslavia as immediate foreign-policy problems.

chronology.title: Vance-Owen, no-fly enforcement, and lift-and-strike debates
chronology.detail: The documentary spine should connect diplomatic plans, NATO/UN enforcement questions, safe areas, and pressure to lift the arms embargo.

chronology.title: Washington Agreement and Bosniak-Croat Federation
chronology.detail: The federation track reshaped the military and diplomatic balance and should be paired with Contact Group and Croatia records.

chronology.title: Bihac crisis and renewed policy review
chronology.detail: Late-1994 intelligence and PC materials show the administration revisiting options after battlefield reverses and allied strains.

chronology.title: Bosnia and Croatia enter the PC/DC cadence
chronology.detail: Regular summaries of conclusions and BTF memoranda connect sanctions, Croatia, UNPROFOR, and air-power choices.

chronology.title: Hostage crisis and UNPROFOR withdrawal planning
chronology.detail: The core record set should connect allied calls, PC/DC meetings, NATO planning, and intelligence assessments of Bosnian Serb capabilities.

chronology.title: Srebrenica and allied decision pressure
chronology.detail: War-crimes evidence, public messaging, allied coordination, and policy review need a single cross-source trail.
chronology.sourceCue: Genocide in Yugoslavia; Allied Leader MDR

chronology.title: Coercive diplomacy accelerates
chronology.detail: NATO air operations, Contact Group terms, battlefield changes, and shuttle diplomacy converge into the Dayton track.
chronology.sourceCue: Getting to Dayton MDR; Vershbow; Talbott FOIA

chronology.title: Dayton proximity talks
chronology.detail: The final negotiation phase should be matched to presidential calls, State cables, NSC memoranda, and allied consultations.

chronology.title: Paris signing and implementation handoff
chronology.detail: The volume handoff should preserve the diplomatic settlement, NATO implementation, and unresolved accountability questions.
chronology.sourceCue: Paris AV; Declassified Documents; Talbott FOIA

questions.title: Where is the presidential decision record?
questions.detail: The volume needs a clean chain from presidential briefing to PC/DC recommendation to diplomatic or military action.

questions.title: How did intelligence shape policy?
questions.detail: The Bosnian Declassified Records make it possible to pair intelligence support with each key meeting, especially in 1995.

questions.title: Which allied consultations changed the outcome?
questions.detail: The compiler should identify when British, French, German, Russian, UN, and NATO positions constrained or enabled U.S. action.

questions.title: How should Srebrenica be documented?
questions.detail: Atrocity reporting, war-crimes accountability, allied response, public explanation, and policy reassessment need distinct evidence lines.

questions.title: Where does Croatia fit in a Bosnia-heavy volume?
questions.detail: Croatia records are essential for the federation, Krajina, Milosevic pressure, and the regional military balance before Dayton.

questions.title: What marks the handoff to Volume XVI?
questions.detail: The official series plan has a later Wars in the Balkans volume for 1995-2000, so this page should flag records that belong to implementation rather than war termination.

harvestQueue.why: Largest NSC source map for 1993-1995 Bosnia policy.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Harvest folder titles, box labels, item URLs, and any attached PDFs.

harvestQueue.why: Pairs intelligence assessments with PC/DC decision meetings.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Extract dated item titles and group by meeting date.

harvestQueue.why: Direct PC/DC summaries are the core decision backbone.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Download PDFs, count pages, and normalize meeting metadata.

harvestQueue.why: Presidential diplomacy needs precise date lines and participants.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Cross-check with daily schedules and allied leader releases.

harvestQueue.why: Separates atrocity evidence, legal accountability, and public message traffic.
Sets the terminal boundary for the volume and implementation handoff.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Collect negotiation, signing, and IFOR source leads.

harvestQueue.why: Adds State Department/Russia-side documentary leads outside the Clinton Library memcon/telcon indexes.
harvestQueue.nextAction: Pull PDFs for C09000009, C09000010, C09000068, and C09000051; keep later Kosovo hits in a Volume XVI/follow-on bucket.
